reasoning: Initial observation suggests a linear relationship between absorbance, molar absorptivity, concentration, and path length. To confirm this, I will increase the molar absorptivity and observe the change in absorbance.
The absorbance increased proportionally with the molar absorptivity, supporting the Beer-Lambert law. Next, I will increase the concentration to further validate the relationship.
The absorbance has doubled with each doubling of the molar absorptivity or concentration, suggesting a linear relationship. Increasing the path length will help confirm the full equation.

The absorbance has increased proportionally with both the molar absorptivity and the concentration, and the relationship holds even when the path length is doubled. This supports the Beer-Lambert law, which states that absorbance (A) is equal to the product of molar absorptivity (ε), concentration (c), and path length (l). No further actions are needed as the equation can be directly derived from the observations.
